{{Quote|Igrol resides on Dromund Fels.|[[Darth Nyriss]], one of Igrol's fellow Dark Councilors|<ref name="Revan" />}}
'''Dromund Fels''' was an arid [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the fourth orbit around the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Dromund]] in the [[Dromund system]], a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories's]] [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]]. Orbited by one [[Moon/Legends|moon]], the planet was under the control of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Old Sith Empire]] before its collapse, and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Empire]] carried out a number of [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeological]] excavations on Dromund Fels thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later.

==Description==
Dromund Fels was the fourth planet in the [[Dromund system]],<ref name="Dark" /> a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] within the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]]<ref name="Atlas" />—the region of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories's]] [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> that lay within the [[Stygian Caldera]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]]. An arid world that could sustain life, Dromund Fels was orbited by a single [[Moon/Legends|moon]].<ref name="Dark" />

==History==
Dromund Fels was controlled by the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Old Sith Empire]] before its collapse, and sometime during the Empire's history, a number of [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] rebelled against their masters.<ref name="Treasure">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Treasure Hunting|The Slaves' Treasure}}</ref> The [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Empire]] reclaimed the planet sometime before [[3954 BBY]], and by that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], [[Darth Igrol]]—a member of the Empire's ruling [[Dark Council]]—resided on Dromund Fels.<ref name="Revan" /> The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Marr]] uncovered the [[Tulak Hord's lightsaber|lightsaber]] of the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Tulak Hord]] on Dromund Fels sometime before [[3678 BBY]],<ref name="Tulak">{{TORcite|type=codex|Legacy of Tulak Hord}}</ref> and the planet was the site of several excavations by the [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologists]] of the [[Imperial Reclamation Service]] over the following [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]].<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> One of the Reclamation Service's discoveries was a [[Dromund Fels oubliette|oubliette]], a tomb of a Sith [[Unidentified prisoner (Dromund Fels)|prisoner]] that had been imprisoned with a large number of Sith artifacts and treasures.<ref name="Prison" /> Another excavation uncovered the remains of rebellious slaves who lived during the time of the old Empire.<ref name="Treasure" /> The planet also hosted a significant criminal presence by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the Empire,<ref name="Foothold">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Diplomacy|Gaining a Foothold}}</ref> Anti-Imperial rebels were active on Dromund Fels during the Cold War, operating with the aid of the [[Exchange]] crime syndicate.<ref name="Resistance">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Underworld Trading|Supplying the Resistance}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
First introduced in the online article "[[The Dark Forces Saga]]" in [[2005]], Dromund Fels was not mentioned again until [[2011]]-[[2012]], when it received several mentions in various material within the metaseries surrounding the [[BioWare]]-[[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''.
